items.headline: Dual-Targeted CAR T Cells Beat Escape, Rejection
items.preview: Researchers have developed a novel CAR T cell therapy that targets both CD19 and CD70, addressing major challenges in cancer treatment.
items.article:
In a groundbreaking advancement poised to redefine the landscape of immunotherapy, researchers have unveiled a novel strategy employing allogeneic CD19 CAR T cells augmented with an anti-rejection CD70 CAR. This innovative approach addresses two of the most substantial obstacles that have hindered the broader application of CAR T cell therapy: antigen escape and alloimmune rejection.
Why this matters: The findings herald a new era in cancer immunotherapy with the potential to significantly improve patient outcomes across hematological malignancies and beyond. The innovation combines CD19-targeting CAR T cells with an additional chimeric antigen receptor directed against CD70, allowing for sustainable tumor targeting and evasion of host immune rejection.

items.headline: Top biotech deals in March 2026
items.preview: March 2026 saw a surge in biotech mergers and acquisitions, with significant deals across various sectors.
items.article:
Biotech deal activity thrived in March 2026, with 12 major mergers and acquisitions announced, surpassing licensing deals. Notable transactions included Lilly's $7.8 billion acquisition of Centessa Pharmaceuticals and Biogen's $5.6 billion purchase of Apellis Pharmaceuticals.
Why this matters: The surge in M&As indicates a robust market for biotech innovation, with companies seeking to enhance their pipelines and address unmet medical needs through strategic acquisitions.

items.headline: Fat Cell microRNA-30a-3p Worsens Liver Fat
items.preview: A new study reveals how microRNA-30a-3p from fat cells exacerbates liver fat accumulation in mice.
items.article:
A groundbreaking study has unveiled a novel mechanism by which adipocyte-derived small extracellular vesicles carry microRNA-30a-3p, significantly aggravating hepatic steatosis in male mice subjected to a high-fat diet. This research sheds light on the intricate cellular crosstalk mediating the progression of fatty liver disease.
Why this matters: Understanding how microRNAs influence liver metabolism could lead to new therapeutic strategies for treating non-alcoholic fatty liver disease, which currently lacks targeted pharmacological treatments.

items.headline: BioNTech reports promising Phase II data for uterine cancer ADC
items.preview: BioNTech's trastuzumab pamirtecan shows significant efficacy in treating advanced uterine cancer.
items.article:
BioNTech has presented compelling Phase II data for its compound trastuzumab pamirtecan, an investigational HER2-targeted antibody-drug conjugate. The results show a confirmed objective response rate of 49.3% in patients with advanced endometrial cancer who had progressed after prior treatments.
Why this matters: These findings support the potential of trastuzumab pamirtecan as a new treatment option for patients with recurrent endometrial cancer, particularly in those with lower HER2 expression levels.

items.headline: Structural insights into subtype-specific agonist recognition by sphingosine-1-phosphate receptors
items.preview: New research provides structural insights into how sphingosine-1-phosphate receptors recognize specific agonists.
items.article:
This study reports four cryo-electron microscopy structures of agonist-bound S1PR1-Gi1 complexes, uncovering the molecular basis for agonist selectivity among S1PR subtypes. The findings highlight nonconserved residues and distinct binding orientations that enhance selectivity for S1PR1 over other subtypes.
Why this matters: Understanding the molecular determinants of agonist selectivity is crucial for the rational design of targeted therapeutic agents, potentially improving treatment outcomes for various diseases.
